title: EA Internal: Flood Warning – River Arrow at Redditch
evidence: Environment Agency activates flood warning for the River Arrow through Redditch following reports of overtopping. EA field team deployed. Gauge data showing extreme flow rates from the upper catchment. Corroborates reports of rising levels.

assessment.justification: EA flood warning for Worcester area corroborates local highways reports. EA gauge data showing extreme flow rates adds hydrological authority to the event assessment. Field team deployment confirms severity level.
assessment.synthesis: EA flood warning active for Worcester city reaches. Gauge data confirms extreme flow. Strongly corroborates local disruption and adds hydrological evidence to the Worcestershire impact.
assessment.confidenceStatement: High confidence. Active EA flood warning with gauge data and field team deployment.

title: EA Internal: Internal Flooding Reported – River Chelt at Cheltenham
evidence: Environment Agency confirms internal flooding at several low-lying properties in Cheltenham as the River Chelt breaches its banks following a rapid rise in gauge levels. EA officers on site assisting residents with flood defences.

assessment.justification: Flood warning at Cheltenham with gauge surpassing 4.5m. EA officer presence confirming internal flooding at multiple properties translates the hydrological signal into a high-confidence tangible impact assessment.
assessment.synthesis: EA confirms property flooding in Cheltenham as River Chelt breaches its banks. Gauge data and officer reports provide authoritative confirmation of significant local impact. Corroborates concurrent highways and social media data.
assessment.confidenceStatement: High confidence. Authoritative EA identification of internal property flooding with officer attendance and gauge data verification.

title: EA Internal: Surface Water Flooding – River Yeo at Yeovil
evidence: Environment Agency reports surface water flooding in low-lying residential areas of Yeovil as the River Yeo overtopped its banks during peak flows. EA field staff deployed along the floodplain to manage water drainage routes. Predicted peak levels indicate continuing risk through the afternoon.

assessment.justification: EA reports of residential surface water flooding in Yeovil are authoritative. Combined with gauge data at 3.2m and staff deployment, this provides a high-confidence picture of tangible impact resulting from the River Yeo's hydrologic peak.
assessment.synthesis: EA confirms surface water flooding in Yeovil residential areas as River Yeo peaks. Staff deployed to manage drainage impacts. High-confidence authoritative signal of significant local community impact in Somerset.
assessment.confidenceStatement: High confidence. Confirmed EA report of residential surface water flooding with gauge data and staff on the ground.

title: EA Internal: Property Flooding Confirmed – River Frome at Stroud
evidence: Environment Agency confirms isolated property flooding and surface water on local roads in the Stroud valley as the River Frome exceeds its design capacity. EA field officers on site clearing blocked culverts and hotspots. 120 properties actively notified of direct flooding currently occurring.

assessment.justification: EA confirmation of property flooding and road impacts in Stroud is a high-confidence signal. Corroborates regional media reporting of Cotswold valley disruption. Field officer presence confirms the shift from warning to tangible physical impact.
assessment.synthesis: EA confirms property and road flooding in the Stroud valley as River Frome breaches its banks. Officers clearing culvert blockages. Corroborates Cotswold regional impact pattern and adds authoritative confirmation of localized property damage.
assessment.confidenceStatement: High confidence. Official EA confirmation of property flooding with gauge data and field officers active on site.
